name: Sierra de la Devesa y vistas del Puntal dels Escambrons — circular desde Almatret
region_intro: Hiking around Almatret offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and reservoir views. The region features trails that traverse agricultural lands, lead along the banks of the Río Ebro, and ascend to viewpoints over the surrounding terrain. These paths provide opportunities to explore the natural environment and historical sites. The area's topography includes moderate elevation changes, making it suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Almatret The most popular hiking route is Views of the Reservoir from Montnegre — circular from Montnegre, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete, offering expansive views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aiguabarreig and Castillo de Mequinenza — circular, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.6 km) path. This route explores the confluence of rivers and passes by the historic Castillo de Mequinenza. Local hikers also love the Sierra Campells — circular along the banks of the Río Ebro, a 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain along the river, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Almatret is defined by riverbank paths, views of reservoirs, and trails through the Sierra Campells and Sierra de la Devesa hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging hikes. The routes in Almatret are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Almatret's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Almatret?
A: Almatret offers a wide selection of routes, with over 25 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Almatret?
A: Yes, Almatret has several easy routes suitable for families. There are 7 trails classified as easy, often featuring gentle terrain along riverbanks or through agricultural lands. These are great for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Almatret area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails around Almatret are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167198140/sierra-de-la-devesa-y-vistas-del-puntal-dels-escambrons-circular-desde-almatret" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Devesa y vistas del Puntal dels Escambrons — circular desde Almatret&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate loop with scenic views.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the hiking trails around Almatret?
A: The region is known for its diverse scenery, including rolling hills, river valleys, and expansive reservoir views. Trails often traverse agricultural lands and ascend to viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086890441/views-of-the-reservoir-from-montnegre-circular-from-montnegre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Reservoir from Montnegre — circular from Montnegre&lt;/a&gt; route is particularly noted for its panoramic reservoir vistas.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes are moderate, Almatret does offer a couple of more challenging hikes for those seeking a greater adventure. These trails typically involve more significant elevation changes and longer distances.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the trails?
A: The area around Almatret is rich in history and natural beauty. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4644675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Ebro in Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086879323/aiguabarreig-and-castillo-de-mequinenza-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aiguabarreig and Castillo de Mequinenza — circular&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic Castillo de Mequinenza.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Almatret?
A: The trails in Almatret are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the peaceful riverbank paths, and the scenic views of the reservoirs and surrounding hills.

Q: Is Almatret suitable for dog-friendly hiking?
A: Yes, the natural landscapes and open trails around Almatret are generally suitable for hiking with dogs. Many paths traverse rural areas and riverbanks, providing ample space for your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local wildlife and agricultural areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almatret?
A: The best times to hike in Almatret are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there trails that follow the Río Ebro?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead along the banks of the Río Ebro, offering unique perspectives of the river and its surrounding ecosystem. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086912056/sierra-campells-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-rio-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Campells — circular along the banks of the Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for experiencing the river up close.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails in Almatret?
A: Parking is generally available near the start points of many trails, especially in or near the village of Almatret and other local access points. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots close to trailheads.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Almatret?
A: Hikes in Almatret vary in length and duration. You can find shorter routes, like the 5.9-mile (9.6 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086879323/aiguabarreig-and-castillo-de-mequinenza-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aiguabarreig and Castillo de Mequinenza — circular&lt;/a&gt; which takes around 2.5 hours, to longer trails such as the 9.0-mile (14.5 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086912056/sierra-campells-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-rio-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Campells — circular along the banks of the Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, which can take over 4 hours.
tips: Border with the province of Zaragoza appears this town located in the Sierra de les Deveses and with the border of the river Ebro on its west side.

The Town Well is located near Camí de les Plaines dels Quadrats. It is a different bridge to the one we normally find, which is said to be from the Saracen period. There are stairs that take you directly to the water's feet.
From this point of the path you can observe the Puntal dels Escambarons and in the background, the town of Riba-roja d'Ebre. Good views of the border area between both provinces.
